item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In Soviet Russia, the bus drives you

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Alright. So for this one its more of a focus on worldbuilding. Reach's Blurprint Folder mentions Anomalous Vehicle Containment Bays, and also links out to numerous other SCPs. Mentioning this object contained in such a bay would not only give the SCPF another notch on some containment standardization, and facilitate some wikiwalking, but ultimatley is rather non-invasive link, changing "standard secure vehicle garage" to "Anomalous Vehicle Containment Bay" As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-1221 is non-hazardous and contained in a standard secure vehicle garage at Site-██. Cleaning and maintenance is to be performed monthly; personnel are only permitted to enter SCP-1221 for these purposes and entrance is restricted to D-class.

To this:

SCP-1221 is non-hazardous and contained in a Anomalous Vehicle Containment Bay at Site-██. Cleaning and maintenance is to be performed monthly; personnel are only permitted to enter SCP-1221 for these purposes and entrance is restricted to D-class.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. Just, yes. My goodness man that was good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was creepy and I enjoyed it. 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why would that be redacted, though? There are plenty of even non-anomolous situations where this can happen (night terrors/sleep paralysis for instance. Ever had a family member prone to them who would yell for help in their sleep that they couldn't move? "Wake up!" "I'm trying…".). They might redact their suspicions about what the vocalizations imply, but there would be no reason to redact the subject of the vocalizations, if that's all it is.

So, I don't think "It's pretty clear that what goes in that spot" is that at all. I'd expect something to be there that they'd actually have a reason to redact.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aw, dangit, I suppose it's a good thing I'm reading through the entire archive before I actually try to submit an SCP of my own, 'cause one of my ideas was almost exactly this.

+1 for Great Minds and all that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, this is the first SCP to legitimately scare me in months. I love the subtlety and the implications, and how the exact nature of the anomaly is left up in the air. Solid upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Delightfuly creepy and well thought, the subtlety is great, but I agree with someone that wrote earlier and said that the "nice try" thing isn't necessary. You are basically killing the subtlety and making it way too obvious.

Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like it. But, as is my way, I am left with questions.
For starters, was Dr. R. ever a passenger? If so, that could explain the O5 note. "Nope, we're not looking into this. Nope. How are you feeling, by the way?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice work, love the reveal. +1 for making me scared of riding the bus each morning. Jerk.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It creeped me out, but it seems to be lacking something i cant put my finger on :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*reads the article*

Enh, whatever, not really thrilling me. Sorry.

*reads the interview*

D8

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Many articles are missing tags, especially older ones, but as long as they have the mandatory tags ([class] & scp), it's not an emergency. Feel free to tag this if you can do it without Mackenzie killing you. (Specifically, if you can do it in such a way that Mackenzie doesn't want to kill you.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article is missing tags.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually… not really.

It's more that the Foundation's reaction to the evidence shown is deeply stupid. The Foundation is not stupider than your average reader. They would devote surveillance to it, even if it showed no results. And they would do something about Dr. R███. And do what they could to contain their employees who apparently might not be in control of themselves.

On further thought, they'd do this regardless of "nice try". In other words, this a pointless way for an otherwise excellent article to shoot itself in the foot.

I dunno. I want to make it work in my head. I guess if the Level 4 stuff is a cover? Maybe because some of the Level 4s reading that info are in fact affected by the bus? And containment of them is a work in progress? Hm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was about to downvote this, but then I read the comments and realized what the hell it was. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely love this. The fridge horror is phenomenal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's pretty clear that what goes in that spot is something along the lines of "not being in control of their own body." I think it's actually an excellent use of engagement, since a little deduction lets you figure it out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Considering the doctor's responses prior to that, it fits his personality perfectly. I mean, he walked in saying that the seizures were a coincidence or some sort of trick, but when they were proven to be an actual occurrence, he gave in and accepted their existence, but still thinks that the interviewer was trying to play a trick on him with the things that he was supposed to have said.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've always thought it was strange how people on buses, trains, and elevators were almost always just absent. Staring blankly forward and not really reacting to anything going on around them until it was time to get off. Now I know it's because public transport is actually a nefarious conduit to a mysterious group of spiritual body-snatchers. Knew it had to be something like that.

+1 and +1/2 more for management not caring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The creep factor is present, but not overdone. Good job author, this is a solid article. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A small number (<10%) of affected subjects are observed to vocalise during seizures (as no memory of the events is retained, consciousness appears to remain impaired), inconsistent with the presentation of typical absence seizures. Generalised EEG discharges are still observed. The vocalisations have a common theme of [DATA EXPUNGED].

The already high amount of redacted notwithstanding, going even further to expunge by far the most potentially interesting bit of the SCP is going to have to net a neg from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yo dawg, we heard you like passengers, so we put a passenger in yo passenger, so you can ride while you ride!

A solid upvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh god this seriously creeps me out (though the 'nice try' bit might be overdoing it a tad… maybe think about cutting that?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Scary thought: the bus isn't anomalous, it never was. The Exposed just faked the path that connected them with the bus, and the real source is something else. That explains how O5-█ was exposed, since he obviously don't use a bus to get to work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The way I understand this, it's a bus that turns you into a passenger…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of that Goosebumps I read when I was a kid, about that mirror that turns you invisible, but sucks you in and replaces you with your "mirrored replica"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: O5-█ rode the bus didn't he

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was going less for the 'crazy bus creating posessed slave army' angle (although I wanted to keep that possibility open) and more like 'it's a normal person but your soul is somewhere else' vaguely like the problem of the teleporter which kills you and creates an exact replica at the destination.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And therefore, perhaps the reason that no effects were seen until a year after the Foundation acquiring the bus is because until then, not enough people were able to resist its effects to be noticeable…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that's what I got too, the people only appear normal, but in fact are being possessed by something. Maybe the bus itself, maybe something else. The ONLY time these people are even in control of themselves are when they experience seizures. The 90% who never experience them are in fact possessed all the time, but the interview indicates that they are fully aware of everything happening and unable to do anything about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Think of it this way: 10% of people on the bus experience a sort of metal takeover of which they can break out of, but with great difficulty ( thus seizure ). However this could also mean 10% of people merely are strong enough to break out of the hold, and in actual fact ALL OF THEM ARE BEING MENTALCONTROLLED BY THE CRAZY BUS.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I *think* it's something to do with the consciousness not having control of the body's actions (the seizure lets the consciousness take over briefly), but I might be wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Uh… i don't quite get this one, though it feels like there's something I should be getting.

Ah well, me and subtlety are two orthogonal concepts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow… if I'm interpreting this SCP correctly I'm very creeped out right now. I particularly like the subtlety.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The remaining 10% are observed to develop a mild absence seizure disorder anywhere from hours to years after exposure.

Is this supposed to mean that the disorder takes hours or years to develop, or that it lasts for hours or years?

Otherwise, pretty good.
